stateKeys.name: status
stateKeys.type: enum: 'not_paid' | 'awaiting' | 'authorized' | 'partially_authorized' | 'canceled' | 'failed' | 'completed' — seven members at v2.5.0; 'failed' is declared but NO code path assigns it (see VALIDATION.md)

stateKeys.name: hasSessions
stateKeys.type: boolean — ABSTRACTION of payment_sessions.length; the derivation only tests === 0

stateKeys.name: authorized
stateKeys.type: enum: 'none' | 'partial' | 'full' — ABSTRACTION of authorizedAmount; the derivation only tests > 0 and >= amount

stateKeys.name: captured
stateKeys.type: enum: 'none' | 'partial' | 'full' — ABSTRACTION of captured_amount; at v2.5.0 this DRIVES the status (captured === amount → completed)

stateKeys.name: refunded
stateKeys.type: enum: 'none' | 'partial' | 'full' — ABSTRACTION of refunded_amount; read by NO status decision at v2.4.0

specialRules.name: no-session-to-authorize
specialRules.note: Unchanged from v2.4.0. Precondition on the SESSION entity, not on collection status.

specialRules.name: nothing-authorized-to-capture
specialRules.note: Unchanged from v2.4.0. capturePayment_ still throws on payment.canceled_at and short-circuits on captured_at.

noOpRule: An action that does not apply is reject(reason) — an observable no-op, never a throw. CANCEL_ORDER still has NO rejection path at v2.5.0: cancelOrderWorkflow stamps every linked collection unconditionally, so even a `completed` collection can be moved to `canceled`.
abstractionBound: Money is abstracted to none/partial/full and session count to a boolean. KNOWN GAP AT v2.5.0: the new clause is `MathBN.eq(amount, capturedAmount)`, an EQUALITY, so a collection with amount === 0 and nothing captured satisfies it and derives `completed` immediately. This abstraction cannot express amount === 0 (its 'none' means 0 captured against a nonzero amount), so that edge case is OUTSIDE the model and is neither checked nor claimed here.
projectionBound: These five keys are the ENTIRE observable state. Provider ids, payment-session data blobs, currency, refund reasons, individual capture/refund records, `completed_at`, metadata, and the whole Order and Cart that own the collection are OUTSIDE the projection: not checked, not migrated, not audited. Every guarantee made here is relative to this list.

upstream.migration: packages/modules/payment/src/migrations/Migration20250207132723.ts — CHECK-constraint widening ONLY; no data migration, no backfill, existing rows keep whatever status they had
